apiVersion: Docs site labeled v1.0; REST paths carry mixed per-endpoint versions (/defi/* unversioned legacy, /defi/v2/*, /defi/v3/*, /wallet/v2/*, /v1/wallet/*). No formal versioning or deprecation policy page documented; changelog (updated every ~3-7 days) shows additive releases rather than breaking changes.

authentication.chainSelectionHeader: x-chain (optional header on most endpoints; default 'solana'; enum per endpoint, superset observed: solana, ethereum, arbitrum, avalanche, bsc, optimism, polygon, base, zksync, monad, hyperevm, aptos, fogo, mantle, megaeth, robinhood, sui)
authentication.websocketAuth: API key as query parameter x-api-key on the wss URL, plus required header 'Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: echo-protocol'
authentication.keyProvisioning: Sign up at https://bds.birdeye.so/auth/sign-up, then generate key in BDS Dashboard > Security tab

baseUrls.purpose: REST API (all plans; no plan-specific hosts documented)
Official Birdeye MCP Server (Beta) for AI agents; authenticated with x-api-key header

endpoints.name: Price (single)
endpoints.freshness: real-time per vendor claim; response carries updateUnixTime of last price update; no quantified latency SLA documented

endpoints.name: Price - Multiple
topLevelResponseFields: data (map keyed by token address -> same price object as /defi/price)
endpoints.freshness: real-time per vendor claim; updateUnixTime per token
endpoints.plans: Lite, Starter, Premium, Business, Enterprise (not free Standard)

endpoints.name: OHLCV V3
optionalParams: x-chain header (subset: solana, bsc, base, ethereum, monad, fogo, mantle, hyperevm, megaeth, robinhood)
topLevelResponseFields: data.items[] (o, h, l, c, v, v_usd, unix_time, address, type, currency)
endpoints.pagination: none; max 5000 candles per request (use time windows to page)
endpoints.freshness: near-real-time (1-second candles offered); retention limits: 1s candles up to 2 weeks, 15s/30s up to 3 months; minute+ retention not documented

endpoints.name: Price - Historical (time series)
endpoints.freshness: historical series; max lookback not documented

endpoints.name: Price - Historical by unix time (point-in-time)
endpoints.freshness: point-in-time historical price at a given unix timestamp (documented use: portfolio reconstruction / backtesting)

endpoints.name: Token - Overview
optionalParams: frames (up to 8 custom intervals: 5s-3600s in 5s steps, 1m-1440m, 1h/2h/4h/8h/24h)
topLevelResponseFields: buy/sell splits and trade counts per frame
endpoints.freshness: rolling-window stats down to 1m (and second-level frames); real-time per vendor claim
endpoints.plans: all tiers incl. free Standard; on Sui market cap and supply fields are unavailable

endpoints.name: Token - Market Data (single)
endpoints.freshness: current snapshot; real-time per vendor claim
endpoints.plans: all tiers incl. free Standard; 'multiple' variant is Business/Enterprise only

endpoints.name: Token - Trade Data (single)
endpoints.freshness: rolling-window trade statistics; real-time per vendor claim
endpoints.cuCost: not individually confirmed (CU table lists Token trade-data family; 'multiple' variant Business+ only)
endpoints.plans: Lite, Starter, Premium, Business, Enterprise (not free Standard)

endpoints.name: Token - Security
topLevelResponseFields: Solana: creatorAddress, ownerAddress, creationTx, creationTime, creatorBalance, creatorPercentage, top10HolderPercent, totalSupply, freezeAuthority, isToken2022, jupStrictList
EVM: isHoneypot, isMintable, isProxy, canTakeBackOwnership, buyTax, sellTax, isAntiWhale, holderCount, lpHolders, totalSupply, creatorAddress, ownerAddress
endpoints.plans: Lite, Starter, Premium, Business, Enterprise (not free Standard); not available on Sui

endpoints.name: Token - List (V3)
optionalParams: min_/max_ filters on liquidity, market_cap, fdv, holder, volume/price-change/trade-count across 1m-30d windows
endpoints.pagination: offset/limit with constraint offset + limit <= 10000; has_next flag; deeper scans require /defi/v3/token/list/scroll (Business/Enterprise only, rate-limited to 2 rps)
endpoints.freshness: screener-style current stats; real-time per vendor claim
endpoints.cuCost: not individually confirmed in fetched CU excerpt
endpoints.plans: all tiers; scroll variant Business/Enterprise only

endpoints.name: Trades - Token Seek By Time
topLevelResponseFields: trade records: quote, base, basePrice, quotePrice, txHash, source, blockUnixTime, txType, owner, side, pricePair, from, to, tokenPrice, poolId
endpoints.pagination: offset/limit (offset + limit <= 10000) plus time-cursor via before_time/after_time; hasNext flag
endpoints.freshness: tick-level DEX trades; real-time per vendor claim
endpoints.cuCost: Trades - Token documented at 10 CU (v3 trades 20 CU)
endpoints.plans: Lite, Starter, Premium, Business, Enterprise (not free Standard)

endpoints.name: Token - Holder list
topLevelResponseFields: data.items[] (amount, decimals, mint, owner, token_account, ui_amount)
endpoints.pagination: offset/limit, capped at 10000 total (top ~10k holders reachable)
endpoints.plans: all tiers; reference page lists supported chain 'Solana' only
endpoints.note: Holder distribution/profile/positions endpoints also exist (holder/v1, token/v1 families)

endpoints.name: Search (token/market)
optionalParams: sort_by (volume_24h_usd default; fdv, marketcap, liquidity, price...)

endpoints.name: Pair - Overview (single)
endpoints.freshness: rolling-window pool stats; real-time per vendor claim

endpoints.name: All-Time Trades (single)
topLevelResponseFields: data: address, total_volume, total_volume_usd, volume_buy_usd, volume_sell_usd, volume_buy, volume_sell, total_trade, buy, sell
endpoints.freshness: cumulative aggregates over token's entire existence ('alltime')
endpoints.cuCost: not individually confirmed; 'multiple' variant Business/Enterprise only

endpoints.name: Token - All Market List
topLevelResponseFields: data.items[] (address, base{address,decimals,symbol,icon}, quote{address,decimals,symbol,icon}, name, source, liquidity, price (nullable), volume24h, trade24h, trade24hChangePercent, uniqueWallet24h, uniqueWallet24hChangePercent, createdAt)
endpoints.freshness: current market/pool stats; real-time per vendor claim
endpoints.plans: all tiers (Standard, Lite, Starter, Premium, Business, Enterprise)

endpoints.name: Wallet - Token List (Portfolio, Beta)
topLevelResponseFields: data.items[] (address, decimals, balance, uiAmount, chainId, name, symbol, icon?, logoURI, priceUsd, valueUsd, isScaledUiToken?, multiplier?)
endpoints.freshness: current portfolio snapshot; response carries no timestamp; real-time per vendor claim
endpoints.plans: Lite, Starter, Premium, Business, Enterprise (not free Standard)
endpoints.note: Reference page is labeled Beta and marked deprecated; a wallet v2 family (current-net-worth, token-balance, transfer) exists. Wallet v1 group is rate-capped at 30 rps/150 rpm (rate-limiting doc also cites a 30 rpm cap on the group).

endpoints.name: Supported Networks
endpoints.cuCost: utils-tier (Utils Credits endpoint also exists at /utils/v1/credits for CU balance checks)

assetClasses: Crypto only: on-chain DEX/token market data (spot AMM trading), token metadata/security, wallet portfolio/PnL, holder analytics, perps analytics (perps/v1 endpoints for perp DEX data), 'smart money' analytics

chains.note: The supported-networks doc page (14 chains) lags the endpoint references (17 x-chain values incl. hyperevm, mantle, robinhood); per-endpoint chain support varies — e.g. OHLCV v3 lists a 10-chain subset, pair overview lists solana/fogo, holder v3 lists Solana only

coverage.marketsExchanges: DEX/AMM venues only; AMM coverage page lists ~229 integrations across 10 chains (e.g. Solana 31, Base 33, Arbitrum 32, BSC 29, Ethereum 24, Polygon 21, Avalanche 16, Optimism 15, Sui 15, zkSync 13); marketing copy says '180+ decentralized exchanges'. No CEX order-book data via the public REST API (an 'Advanced CEX liquidity' doc page exists for the Birdeye app, not documented as a BDS API).
instrumentTypes: No equities, FX, rates, fixed income, or traditional asset classes

freshness.restPrices: Vendor states BDS provides 'high-quality, real-time blockchain and DeFi data'; price responses include updateUnixTime; no quantified latency SLA documented
freshness.ohlcv: Candles down to 1-second granularity (implies near-real-time ingestion); no explicit latency figure documented
freshness.trades: Tick-level DEX transactions with blockUnixTime; 'Txs - Latest Block Number' endpoint exposes ingestion head; no latency SLA documented
freshness.websocket: Streaming subscriptions (price/OHLCV, trades, new listings, new pairs, large trades, wallet txs, token stats, transfers) for push delivery; no latency SLA documented
freshness.eodOrDelayed: No delayed/EOD product tiers documented — all plans appear to serve the same live data; free Standard tier is limited by access/rate, not by delay (delay-based tiering not documented)

historicalDepth.ohlcvSecondCandles: Documented retention: 1s candles up to 2 weeks; 15s and 30s candles up to 3 months
historicalDepth.trades: seek_by_time supports arbitrary before_time/after_time cursors; total addressable depth not documented; all-time trade aggregates available via /defi/v3/all-time/trades ('alltime' time_frame)

pagination.primaryMechanism: offset/limit query params with hard cap 'offset + limit <= 10000' on list-style endpoints (token list, holders, trades); has_next/hasNext boolean in responses
pagination.timeCursor: trade endpoints add before_time/after_time unix-second cursors ('seek_by_time' family) to page beyond the offset cap
pagination.scroll: 'Token - List (V3) Scroll' endpoint exists for deep enumeration; Business/Enterprise only; rate-limited to 2 rps
pagination.batchEndpoints: multi/multiple/batch variants take comma-separated address lists (e.g. multi_price max 100 addresses) instead of pagination

perApiCaps.midGroup100rps: most trade, OHLCV, token, pair, holder, mint/burn endpoints
perApiCaps.walletV1Group: 30 rps / 150 rpm on /v1/wallet/token_list, /v1/wallet/tx_list, /v1/wallet/token_balance, /v1/wallet/list_supported_chain; rate-limiting doc also states a 30 rpm cap on the wallet API group

rateLimits.quotas: Usage metered in Compute Units (CUs) per plan (30k free ... 500M on B-50); overage billed per additional 1M CUs ($23 Lite down to $3.50 B-50); dynamic CU pricing for OHLCV v3 (60/90/120 CU by candle count) and recent-txs (25/75/125 CU by rows); WebSocket usage billed as Compute Unit Per Byte (CUPB 0.00008-0.08 by subscription type); /utils/v1/credits endpoint checks remaining CUs
rateLimits.backoffGuidance: Docs recommend implementing 'exponential backoff or retry-after mechanisms to retry failed requests after a delay'; no Retry-After or X-RateLimit-* response headers documented

errors.envelope: {"success": false, "message": "<description>"} — same success/message envelope as successful responses (success:true + data)

statusCodes.400: Bad Request — invalid request parameters or payload
statusCodes.403: Forbidden — access denied (no permission for the resource, e.g. plan entitlement)
statusCodes.429: Too Many Requests — rate limit exceeded; wait and retry

entitlements.model: Two axes: (1) monthly CU quota + rps by plan; (2) endpoint access matrix by package ('Data accessibility by packages')
entitlements.freeStandardTier: Single-item price/OHLCV/overview/market-data, token lists, holders, trending, search, most single-lookup endpoints; excludes multi_price, token_security, token_creation_info, metadata, trade-data, seek_by_time trade paging, wallet portfolio
entitlements.liteStarter: Adds token_security, creation info, metadata (single), trade-data (single), seek_by_time trades, wallet portfolio/tx history, multi_price; docs state 'The LITE/STARTER package can only access the multiple price API; all other multiple APIs are not accessible'
entitlements.premium: Adds WebSockets (per pricing and accessibility pages) and Token Meta Data (Multiple)
entitlements.businessEnterprise: All APIs; exclusively unlocks batch/multi endpoints (market-data multiple, trade-data multiple, price stats multiple, pair overview multiple, wallet net-worth/PnL multiple, holder batch, all-time trades multiple, token list scroll, token list all)
entitlements.websocketDiscrepancy: Pricing page: Premium includes websockets with 500 connections, Business 2,000; accessibility matrix: websocket subscriptions marked Premium and Business/Enterprise; websocket doc page: 'WebSocket access is available for Business Package subscribers, which includes 500 concurrent connections' — inconsistent across official pages

sdks.name: Official MCP Server (mcp.birdeye.so/mcp)
sdks.language: protocol-level (MCP); connect via npx mcp-remote with x-api-key header

sdks.name: Language SDKs (Python/JS/etc.)
sdks.status: No official client libraries documented on docs.birdeye.so; third-party wrappers (e.g. birdeye-py) exist but are not official and were not used as sources

licensing.source: Birdeye Terms of Service at docs.birdeye.so/docs/terms (Wings Lab entity)
licensing.storageCaching: ToS Clause 10.2.a prohibits users from 'copy, scrape, extract, download, store, reproduce, replicate, record, or otherwise capture' data, including via 'bots, crawlers, scripts, APIs, or any other tools' — as written this appears platform-oriented, but no separate API/BDS data-license carve-out permitting storage/caching is documented
licensing.redistribution: Prohibited: 'resell, sublicense, lease, rent, distribute, transfer, share, or otherwise provide access to the Services' (10.2.c); 'You shall not deep-link, redistribute or facilitate the redistribution of Dashboard Information' (4.5)
licensing.attribution: No attribution or display requirements documented (a brand-assets page exists but no mandated attribution found)
licensing.permittedUse: Services 'for your personal own use' (7.2.h); dashboard information 'not intended for actual trading purposes or market advice' (4.6)
licensing.termination: Termination 'with or without notice' for violations or 'any other reasons' (11.2); no refunds post-termination (11.1)
licensing.institutionalImplication: The public ToS as written conflicts with institutional needs (storing data, serving derived data to internal users/clients). Redistribution/storage rights would need to be secured contractually via the documented Enterprise channel (docs enterprise-contact page) before production use

limitations: Crypto/DEX-only: no equities, FX, fixed income, commodities, or CEX order books — cannot serve as a multi-asset provider; it is a specialist on-chain data source
No quantified latency SLA or uptime guarantee documented anywhere in public docs; 'real-time' is a marketing-level claim
Historical depth undocumented for most datasets (only second-level OHLCV retention is specified: 1s = 2 weeks, 15s/30s = 3 months); institutional backtesting depth cannot be verified from docs

Per-chain feature asymmetry: Sui lacks wallet APIs, token_security, token_creation_info, tokens/all, and market-cap/supply fields; liquidity is computed differently on Sui; several v3 endpoints list only chain subsets (holder v3: Solana only; pair overview: solana/fogo)
Docs internally inconsistent in places: supported-networks page (14 chains) vs endpoint x-chain enums (17); websocket plan entitlement stated differently on three official pages
Pagination hard cap offset + limit <= 10000 on list/holder/trade endpoints; deep enumeration requires Business/Enterprise scroll endpoint throttled to 2 rps

Identifier regime is contract addresses per chain, not symbols/ISINs — symbol mapping and cross-chain entity resolution are the integrator's responsibility; search endpoint is fuzzy/symbol-based and Solana-only for verify_token filter
Free Standard tier is 1 rps / 30k CU — unusable beyond evaluation; batch endpoints locked to Business+ raises cost of portfolio-scale polling on lower tiers
No machine-readable error codes (message strings only); no documented Retry-After / X-RateLimit-* headers, complicating precise client-side throttling
No formal versioning/deprecation policy; v1/v2/v3 endpoint families coexist and evolve via changelog — integration requires active changelog monitoring
Token security/rug-check fields differ structurally between Solana and EVM chains; no methodology or revision policy documented
ToS as published prohibits storing/redistributing data, and states data is 'not intended for actual trading purposes' — a hard legal blocker absent an enterprise agreement

primaryFor: On-chain DEX market data across Solana + major EVM chains + Sui/Aptos: real-time token prices, tick-level DEX trades, OHLCV down to 1s, pool liquidity — this is Birdeye's core institutional value
Token screening/discovery (token list v3 with rich filters, new listings, trending) and long-tail/memecoin coverage (~8M+ tokens claimed via MCP page)
Token security/rug-check screening for pre-trade diligence on long-tail tokens (non-Sui chains)
Wallet analytics: portfolio net worth, PnL, holder distribution, top traders, smart-money flows
fallbackFor: Cross-chain token reference data / metadata (fallback to dedicated metadata providers; address-keyed, symbol mapping DIY)
Historical crypto price series for majors (fallback where a provider with documented depth is primary, since Birdeye lookback is undocumented)
notSuitableFor: Any non-crypto asset class (equities, FX, rates, commodities)
CEX spot/derivatives order-book or trade data
Regulated/auditable EOD valuation feeds requiring documented methodology, revision policy, and SLA
hardConstraints: Do not present Birdeye data as exchange-official or SLA-backed real-time; no latency guarantee is documented
Do not persist, cache long-term, or redistribute Birdeye data (including to platform end-users) without an enterprise license — public ToS prohibits it
ToS states data is not intended for actual trading purposes; trading-adjacent use requires contractual cover
Batch endpoints and websockets require Premium/Business+ tiers; capacity planning must use the CU model (incl. dynamic CU on OHLCV v3 and recent txs)
1s/15s/30s OHLCV must be consumed and archived within the 2-week/3-month retention windows if needed later — but note archival itself is a licensing question
Per-chain capability matrix must gate feature exposure (e.g. hide token security for Sui)

sourcesConsulted.whatItVerified: Official docs root; nav structure (Quick Start, Public/Premium APIs, BDS, MCP Server); docs version label v1.0; llms.txt index availability

Complete official index of documentation and API reference pages; endpoint families (price, OHLCV, token, pair, txs, wallet, holder, smart money, perps, blockchain, websocket); changelog entries incl. July 2026 releases
API key provisioning flow via bds.birdeye.so dashboard Security tab; key passed in request headers
Per-tier account rate limits (Standard 1 rps ... Business 100 rps/1500 rpm, Enterprise custom); wallet API group 30 rpm cap; exponential backoff recommendation
Per-endpoint rate caps: 300 rps group, 100 rps group, tokenlist/security 150 rps, token list scroll 2 rps, wallet v1 endpoints 30 rps/150 rpm

Plan tiers and prices (Standard $0/30k CU/1 rps; Lite $39; Starter $99; Premium $199/websockets/50 rps; Business $499-$2,050 tiers; Growth AutoScale; Enterprise custom); CU overage rates; websocket connection counts (Premium 500, Business 2,000) and CUPB overage note

Endpoint-by-package entitlement matrix; batch/multiple endpoints exclusive to Business+; Lite/Starter multiple-API restriction; websocket subscriptions at Premium+; token_security not on free Standard

CU costs per endpoint (price 3, history_price 45, historical_price_unix 6, OHLCV 35, token_overview 25, metadata 5, market-data 12, token_security 40, trades token 10, v3 trades 20, wallet net worth 40, holder 40); batch and dynamic CU references; websocket CUPB 0.00008-0.08
Dynamic CU tables: OHLCV v3 60/90/120 CU by candle count (max 5000); recent txs 25/75/125 CU by rows (max 500)
14 supported networks listed; Sui limitations (no wallet APIs, token_security, token_creation_info, tokens/all; missing market cap/supply in overview; different liquidity calc)
'180+ decentralized exchanges' coverage claim; 10-chain list with staleness disclaimer; absence of documented historical depth/latency on this page
Per-chain AMM/DEX integration lists (~229 total across 10 chains: Solana 31, Base 33, Arbitrum 32, BSC 29, Ethereum 24, Polygon 21, Avalanche 16, Optimism 15, Sui 15, zkSync 13)

ToS clauses: 10.2.a capture/storage prohibition; 10.2.c resale/redistribution prohibition; 4.5 no deep-linking/redistribution of dashboard information; 7.2.h personal use; 4.6 not for actual trading purposes; 11.1/11.2 termination; no attribution mandate found

WebSocket URL format wss://public-api.birdeye.so/socket/{chain}?x-api-key=...; Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: echo-protocol; subscription types; 100 tokens/connection (price/txs/ohlcv), 1 address/connection (wallet); 'Business Package ... 500 concurrent connections' statement (conflicts with pricing page)
Error envelope {success:false, message:string}; status codes 400/401/403/422/429/500 with messages; no Retry-After header or formal backoff spec
'high-quality, real-time blockchain and DeFi data' freshness claim; 'enterprise-ready platform' positioning; absence of latency/uptime SLAs
Official Birdeye MCP Server (Beta) at https://mcp.birdeye.so/mcp; x-api-key auth; 15k+ coins / 8M+ tokens claims; no official language SDKs documented
Release cadence (~every 3-7 days); July 2026 releases (Robinhood chain, Blockchain Data APIs, First-Buyers API, Token Liquidity History APIs); additive evolution, no formal deprecation notices
Base URL https://public-api.birdeye.so; X-API-KEY header; x-chain header 17-value enum; /defi/price params and response schema
/defi/multi_price params, 100-address max, response map schema, plan availability (not free tier)
/defi/v3/ohlcv params (18 interval types incl. 1s/15s/30s), time range mode, 5000-candle cap, second-candle retention (1s: 2 weeks; 15s/30s: 3 months), candle response fields, 10-chain subset
/defi/history_price params (address_type token|pair, intervals 1m-1M, time_from/time_to), response items schema, no documented lookback limit
/defi/historical_price_unix point-in-time price lookup, params and response schema, backtesting use case
/defi/token_overview params (frames up to 8 custom intervals incl. second-level), full stats response fields (marketCap, fdv, supplies, liquidity, holder, per-frame volume/price-change/unique-wallet)
/defi/token_security params; Solana vs EVM response field sets; all chains except Sui; Lite+ plan requirement
/defi/v3/token/market-data params and response (price, liquidity, supplies, market_cap, fdv, holder); 17-chain support; all-tier access
/defi/v3/token/trade-data/single params (frames max 8) and per-frame price/volume/trade/unique-wallet response fields; Lite+ requirement
/defi/v3/token/list sort/filter params, offset+limit<=10000 pagination, has_next, 100/call cap
/defi/txs/token/seek_by_time params (tx_type, before_time/after_time), trade record fields, hasNext, offset cap
/defi/v3/token/holder params, pagination cap, holder item fields, Solana-only chain listing
/defi/v3/search params (search_by/search_mode/target/chain/markets/verify_token), limit 1-20
/defi/v3/pair/overview/single params and pool stats response fields; solana/fogo chain listing
/defi/v3/all-time/trades/single time_frame enum incl. 'alltime', cumulative volume/trade response fields, 17-chain support
/defi/networks response (array of network strings); endpoint-level error status codes 400/401/403/429/500

/defi/v2/markets params (address, sort_by liquidity|volume24h, sort_type, offset, limit 1-20 default 10), 17-value x-chain enum, market item response fields (base/quote token objects, name, source, liquidity, nullable price, volume24h, trade24h(+ChangePercent), uniqueWallet24h(+ChangePercent), createdAt), data.total, all-tier availability

/v1/wallet/token_list params (wallet required, ui_amount_mode raw|scaled), x-chain solana-only, full response schema (data.wallet, data.totalUsd, data.items[] address/decimals/balance/uiAmount/chainId/name/symbol/icon/logoURI/priceUsd/valueUsd/isScaledUiToken/multiplier), verbatim example response, Lite+ plan requirement, Beta/deprecated labeling

openQuestions: WebSocket entitlement: pricing page says Premium includes websockets (500 connections), accessibility matrix marks websocket subs Premium+, but the websocket doc page says access is 'available for Business Package subscribers' — which is authoritative?

Do BDS/enterprise API agreements grant storage, caching, derived-works, and redistribution rights that override the public ToS clauses 10.2.a/10.2.c/4.5? Public docs provide only an enterprise-contact channel, no API data license text
Historical lookback for minute+ OHLCV, history_price, tick trades, and wallet PnL is undocumented — actual depth must be confirmed with the vendor before committing to backtesting workloads
No latency/uptime SLA documented — what SLA do Enterprise contracts offer?

Exact CU cost for several institutional endpoints (token list v3, search, pair overview, trade-data single, all-time trades) not confirmed from the fetched CU table excerpt — consult the full compute-unit-cost and batch-token-cu-cost pages when contracting
Chain list inconsistency (supported-networks page: 14 chains vs endpoint enums: 17 incl. hyperevm, mantle, robinhood) — confirm production-supported chain set and per-endpoint chain matrix
Whether the 429 response ever includes a Retry-After header is undocumented
Price/OHLCV methodology (pool-weighting, outlier filtering beyond the 'outlier' flag, wash-trade handling) is not documented — material for research-grade data quality assessment
Perps v1 and smart-money endpoints appear recent; their tier requirements and stability guarantees need confirmation
